name: ShippingEasy
what_it_does: Multi-carrier shipping management platform with discounted USPS/UPS/FedEx rates, order import, label printing, tracking, and shipment automation for e-commerce sellers.
use_cases: Automated label generation from imported store orders
Multi-carrier rate shopping at discounted rates
Bulk shipment processing for e-commerce fulfillment
Shipment tracking and status sync back to store platforms
not_for: International customs brokerage beyond basic forms
Pure API use without account-based discounted rates
best_when: Small to mid-size e-commerce sellers wanting discounted USPS rates and multi-carrier label printing without building carrier integrations from scratch.
avoid_when: You need a pure programmatic API with no account portal dependency, or require freight/LTL capabilities.

auth.notes: API key plus HMAC-SHA256 request signing. Single key per account. No granular scopes or token rotation documented.

pricing.notes: Subscription tiers based on monthly shipment volume. USPS Commercial Plus rates included. Carrier fees billed separately.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic HTTP status codes returned with minimal error detail. Some endpoints return generic 422 responses without field-level specifics.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Agents must deduplicate on their side using order IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signing is required on all requests — easy to get wrong with non-standard implementations
Carrier account linking must be done via portal before API use
Pagination uses page/per_page params but max per_page is undocumented and varies by endpoint
Webhook delivery is not guaranteed; no retry policy documented
Rate limit headers not consistently returned

security_score_components.security_notes: HMAC signing adds request integrity. However, no scope granularity and no key rotation documented. Single API key model is a liability if compromised.
